title: Breakdown
wikitext:
[]: Breakdown!             Quote Post by Iblis 19th Angel » Fri Sep 09, 2005 3:51 am Has anybody ever played Breakdown by Namco & noticed the similarities to Eva? The enemies in the game, the T'Lan, are creatures that posess forcefields that are impenitrable to bullets & can only be defeated by someone who has been artificially enhanced with their powers. They emerged from an underground cavern buried under Japan for millions of years that even has artificial daylight & forests. The cavern even contains a mummified giant humanoid monster. One of the supporting characters is a female scientist who wears a labcoat everywhere & even has a top with a zipper that has a ring on it. In another possible mecha anime reference, her pet cat is named Casval, which is the real name of Gundam's Char "Red Comet" Aznable. The main antagonist, Solus (Soulless?), is a T'lan with humanlike features & long-ish, spiky silver hair. Interesting, eh? There are tons of Eva inspired manga, anime & games out there (RahXephon, Brainpowered, Betterman, Xenoseries) but it's odd to see one that doesn't really involve giant biomecha...

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Sep 28, 2005 9:50 pm http://breakdown.namco.com/ Interesting. I love seeing where influences and currents of ideas are taken. If it really is as much like Eva as you say, I should take a look at the game.
